title: Guided Power BI Dashboard Building (User-Claude Collaboration)
description: Build portfolio-quality Power BI dashboard pages through step-by-step collaboration. Claude provides instructions, user executes in Power BI, then Claude reviews screenshots and provides feedback. This interactive approach ensures quality visuals while overcoming browser automation limitations.

task_verification.notes: Pre-migration task — verification predates current schema.

acceptanceCriteria: Page 1 (Executive Dashboard) has 4 KPI cards + at least 2 charts
Page 2 (Risk & Sustainability) has 3 KPI cards + at least 2 charts
All visuals display data correctly (no errors)
Professional layout with proper spacing and alignment
notes: - Page 1 (Executive Dashboard): 4 KPIs + Column Chart + Donut Chart
- Page 2 (Risk & Sustainability): 2 KPIs + EPI Bar Chart + Spend Bar Chart
- Created new semantic model (OEMInsightBI_v2) with proper DirectLake relationships
- Fixed gold_dim_material duplicate key issue in silver-to-gold2.Notebook
KEY INSIGHT: High-spend countries (Malaysia, Chile, China, DRC) are NOT in top EPI performers

collaborativeWorkflow.description: Step-by-step interactive workflow between Claude and User

steps.action: Provide specific instructions for creating a visual
steps.example: Click on empty canvas area, then click 'Clustered column chart' icon in Visualizations pane

steps.action: Execute the instructions in Power BI
steps.example: User performs the clicks in the browser

steps.action: Return to Claude and say 'Done' or describe any issues
steps.example: User types: 'Done, chart is created but empty'

steps.action: Take screenshot using Playwright browser_take_screenshot tool
Review screenshot and provide feedback + next instructions
steps.example: Claude says: 'Good, now drag Total Spend EUR to Y-axis well'

steps.action: Repeat steps 1-5 until visual is complete, then move to next visual
steps.example: Continue until all visuals on page are done

page2_risk_sustainability.config: Total Spend by Country by country_name_std
